title: Fix example in `populate_by_name` documentation
body:
## Change Summary

fix the docstring of ConfigDict's populate_by_name.

## Related issue number

fix #11765 

- #11765

step_plan.summary: Fix the incorrect `populate_by_name` documentation example in `pydantic/config.py` so it correctly shows populating a model by alias and by field name, matching the intended behavior and resolving #11765.

steps.operation: Correct the docstring example under `ConfigDict.populate_by_name` to demonstrate population by alias vs population by field name (fix the duplicated `Model(my_alias='foo')` call, updating the second to use the field name, e.g. `Model(my_field='foo')`) and ensure the numbered comments match the code shown.
steps.reason: Issue #11765 reports the example is internally inconsistent and misleading; fixing the example clarifies how `populate_by_name` affects whether input data can be provided using the field name in addition to the alias.
steps.side_effects: Documentation output generated via mkdocstrings/MkDocs will change; no runtime behavior changes. If the project runs docstring/doctest-style validation, the updated snippet must remain syntactically valid and aligned with actual alias/name semantics.
